chapterSummary.title: Our Environment
chapterSummary.content: This chapter helps students explore the world around them by understanding living and non-living things and how they work together in ecosystems. Students will learn to identify the characteristics of living things such as growth, breathing, movement, and reproduction. They will also discover how plants, animals, and non-living things like water, air, and sunlight are all connected in an ecosystem through food chains and habitats.
keyPoints: Living things grow, breathe, eat, move, feel, and reproduce
Non-living things do not have these characteristics
An ecosystem is a community of living and non-living things interacting together
Food chains show how energy flows from producers to consumers to decomposers
Every living thing has a habitat that provides food, water, and shelter
learningObjectives: Identify and classify things as living or non-living
List the characteristics of living things
Understand what an ecosystem is and name its parts
Explain how a food chain works
Describe how living things adapt to their habitats

curatedNotes.title: Quick Sorting Activity
curatedNotes.content: Look around your room and make two lists: one for living things and one for non-living things. Remember, something that was once alive (like a wooden table) is still classified as non-living because it can no longer grow, breathe, or reproduce. Try sorting at least 10 things!

curatedNotes.title: Draw Your Own Food Chain
curatedNotes.content: Practice drawing a food chain from your local environment. Start with a plant (producer), then an animal that eats the plant (herbivore), then an animal that eats that animal (carnivore). For example: Grass -> Grasshopper -> Frog -> Snake -> Eagle. Add arrows to show the direction energy flows.

curatedNotes.title: Common Mistake to Avoid
curatedNotes.content: Many students think that non-living things that move (like a car or a river) are living. Remember: living things move on their own because they are alive. A car needs a person to drive it, and a river flows because of gravity. Always check all characteristics, not just movement!
